what makes Rust memory-safe without a garbage collector
what makes Rust memory-safe without a garbage collector?
what makes Rust memory-safe without a garbage collector?
Rust achieves memory safety through three mechanisms:
— one owner per value; freed when owner goes out of scope.
— shared (&T) or exclusive (&mut T), never both at once.
— compiler tracks reference validity, no dangling pointers.
Result: no use-after-free, no data races, zero runtime cost.
